DeepSeek API Cost Calculator

Calculate DeepSeek API costs for V4 Flash, V4 Pro, and legacy R1. Includes automatic caching, growth projections, and cross-provider savings comparison.

🎭 Scenario Presets
📝 Token Estimator
Estimated: ~<span id="est-tokens">0</span> input tokens (~1.3 w/t EN, ~0.65 c/t CJK)
⚙️ Advanced Options (Caching, Growth)
Available: deepseek-v4-flash, deepseek-v4-pro-promo, deepseek-v4-pro, deepseek-r1

How to Use This Tool

1
Select the DeepSeek models you want to compare (V4 Flash, V4 Pro, etc.).
2
Enter your average input and output tokens per API call.
3
Enter your expected daily request volume.
4
Set the auto-cache hit rate — DeepSeek automatically caches repeated prefixes.
5
Add a growth rate and projection period for long-term cost planning.
6
Review the cost comparison and see how much you save vs OpenAI, Claude, and Gemini.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much cheaper is DeepSeek than OpenAI?

DeepSeek V4 Flash at $0.14/$0.28 per 1M tokens is approximately 95-98% cheaper than GPT-4o ($2.50/$10). For a typical 1,000-token prompt at 100 reqs/day: DeepSeek costs ~$0.84/month vs $22.50/month for GPT-4o. V4 Pro at promo pricing is still 90% cheaper than GPT-4o.

What is DeepSeek Automatic Caching?

DeepSeek automatically caches repeated prefix tokens (system prompts, tool schemas) with no code changes needed. Cache reads get a 98% discount (0.02x input price). On V4 Flash, cached tokens cost just $0.0028/MTok instead of $0.14/MTok. This is fully automatic — no cache write configuration required.

Which DeepSeek model should I use?

V4 Flash ($0.14/$0.28) for: general chat, RAG, code completion, classification. V4 Pro (promo $0.44/$0.87) for: complex reasoning, multi-step logic, competitive coding. V4 Flash handles 90% of use cases at the lowest price. Use Pro only when you need the strongest reasoning.

Is DeepSeek quality comparable to GPT-4o?

DeepSeek V4 Flash matches GPT-4o on most benchmarks at 1/18 the output price. V4 Pro competes with GPT-5.2/Opus 4.8 for reasoning tasks. The main gap is multimodal (image/vision) capabilities where OpenAI and Claude lead. For text-only tasks, DeepSeek is the most cost-effective by far.

How do I migrate from legacy R1/V3 to V4?

Change your model parameter: deepseek-chat → deepseek-v4-flash, deepseek-reasoner → deepseek-v4-pro. The legacy aliases will be retired July 24, 2026. Same base URL and API key — just update the model name. The API format is OpenAI-compatible.

Does DeepSeek have rate limits?

DeepSeek offers generous rate limits for pay-as-you-go users. During peak hours, free-tier users may experience longer queue times. Paid users get priority access. The API is OpenAI-compatible, making migration trivial from any OpenAI-based codebase.

Is DeepSeek safe for production use?

DeepSeek hosts all models in US data centers with enterprise-grade security. For regulated industries, check data residency requirements. Many enterprises use DeepSeek for cost-sensitive workloads while keeping OpenAI/Claude for sensitive data. Always review the latest privacy policy for your use case.

× $2.00/1M → $0.0010/req ──────────────────────────────────────────── Per request: $0.0016 Daily (100): $0.16 Monthly (30d): $4.65 Annual: $55.80 ──────────────────────────────────────────── 💰 Savings
